data.content: No conflicts here — each package appears in only one project. I'll create `Directory.Packages.props` and strip `Version` from all `.csproj` files.

arguments.file_text:
<Project>
  <PropertyGroup>
    <ManagePackageVersionsCentrally>true</ManagePackageVersionsCentrally>
  </PropertyGroup>
  <ItemGroup>
    <PackageVersion Include="Microsoft.AspNetCore.OpenApi" Version="8.0.24" />
    <PackageVersion Include="Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk" Version="17.12.0" />
    <PackageVersion Include="OpenTelemetry.Extensions.Hosting" Version="1.15.0" />
    <PackageVersion Include="System.Text.Json" Version="9.0.0" />
    <PackageVersion Include="xunit" Version="2.9.3" />
    <PackageVersion Include="xunit.runner.visualstudio" Version="2.8.2" />
  </ItemGroup>
</Project>

arguments.old_str:
<PackageReference Include="Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk" Version="17.12.0" />
    <PackageReference Include="xunit" Version="2.9.3" />
    <PackageReference Include="xunit.runner.visualstudio" Version="2.8.2" PrivateAssets="all" />

data.reasoningText:
Now I have a clear picture. Let me create the Central Package Management setup:

Packages across all projects:
- System.Text.Json 9.0.0 (Core)
- OpenTelemetry.Extensions.Hosting 1.15.0 (Web)
- Microsoft.AspNetCore.OpenApi 8.0.24 (Web)
- Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk 17.12.0 (Tests)
- xunit 2.9.3 (Tests)
- xunit.runner.visualstudio 2.8.2 (Tests) - has PrivateAssets="all"


Since there are no version conflicts, I'll set up the central management by creating a Directory.Packages.props file at the solution root with centralized version management enabled and all package versions defined there, then strip the version attributes from each project's package references while preserving other attributes like PrivateAssets.

arguments.file_text:
<Project>
  <PropertyGroup>
    <ManagePackageVersionsCentrally>true</ManagePackageVersionsCentrally>
  </PropertyGroup>
  <ItemGroup>
    <PackageVersion Include="Microsoft.AspNetCore.OpenApi" Version="8.0.24" />
    <PackageVersion Include="Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk" Version="17.12.0" />
    <PackageVersion Include="OpenTelemetry.Extensions.Hosting" Version="1.15.0" />
    <PackageVersion Include="System.Text.Json" Version="9.0.0" />
    <PackageVersion Include="xunit" Version="2.9.3" />
    <PackageVersion Include="xunit.runner.visualstudio" Version="2.8.2" />
  </ItemGroup>
</Project>

    <PackageReference Include="xunit" Version="2.9.3" />
    <PackageReference Include="xunit.runner.visualstudio" Version="2.8.2" PrivateAssets="all" />

data.content:
Done. Here's what I did:

1. **Created `Directory.Packages.props`** at the solution root with `ManagePackageVersionsCentrally` enabled and all 6 package versions centralized.

2. **Removed `Version` attributes** from `PackageReference` entries in all 3 `.csproj` files (Core, Web, Tests), preserving other attributes like `PrivateAssets="all"`.

No version conflicts existed — each package appeared in only one project.
